howItWorks.intro: This exposure calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Equivalent Exposure: EV stays constant when stops balance out
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is exposure value (EV)?
faq.answer: EV is a single number representing a combination of aperture, shutter speed, and ISO. EV 0 equals f/1.0 at 1 second at ISO 100. Each +1 EV halves the light reaching the sensor.

faq.question: How do I use the exposure triangle?
faq.answer: The three settings — aperture, shutter speed, and ISO — are interconnected. Changing one requires adjusting another to maintain the same exposure. Opening the aperture by 1 stop lets you double the shutter speed.

faq.question: What EV is needed for outdoor photography?
faq.answer: Bright sunny days are EV 14-16. Overcast skies are EV 10-12. Sunset/sunrise is EV 8-10. Night photography typically requires EV 0 or below.
